method_atom: A perturbative model that expresses one observable field as a local Taylor-series expansion in an underlying field, with the expansion coefficients treated as free parameters. The model is enriched to depend on additional tensorial structures built from second derivatives of a potential and of a flow field, and symmetry arguments together with equivalences among candidate terms are used to collapse the many possible terms down to a minimal set of independent free parameters needed to compute low-order correlation statistics (two- and three-point spectra) up to a fixed order in the underlying perturbations. Short-range non-locality is captured by a controlled series of higher-derivative correction terms governed by a characteristic length scale, small as long as that scale is below the probed resolution.
object_atom: The spatial clustering of galaxies (or other dark matter tracers) relative to the underlying cosmic mass-density field on very large scales, as probed by future high-precision redshift surveys of large-scale structure.
brief_reason: What carries across is the symmetry-constrained local-expansion-with-free-coefficients machinery and its higher-derivative non-locality corrections for predicting correlation statistics; what is domain-specific is galaxies tracing the cosmic density field in redshift surveys.
